sections.label: Intended Use Statement
sections.excerpt: Underwriting risk score is intended for use as a decision-support signal in unsecured and secured consumer credit underwriting. Output is a normalized probability-of-default score in [0,1]; deployers are responsible for setting decision thresholds and human-review gates.

sections.label: Training Data Summary
sections.excerpt: Trained on 41M anonymized consumer loan applications from 2018–2024 across 142 deployer customers. Geographic coverage: all 50 states + DC. Demographic distribution provided as Appendix B.

sections.label: Known Limitations
sections.excerpt: Calibration drift observed on applications from borrowers with thin-file credit histories (<24 months bureau data). Quarterly recalibration recommended. Performance not validated on non-English application narratives.

sections.label: Risk Mitigation Practices
sections.excerpt: Pre-deployment fairness testing across protected-class proxies; quarterly drift monitoring; reviewer escalation path for borderline-band decisions.

sections.label: Trade-Secret Withholding Notice
sections.excerpt: Feature-weight matrix and training-data composition by bureau source withheld as trade secret per §1702(1); deployer notified per §1702(1)(d).

pending_diff.summary: nCino has published Underwriting-v4.3 — material update to the employment-tenure feature weight. New §1702 packet attached.

sections.label: Intended Use Statement
sections.excerpt: Pre-qualification optimizer is intended to personalize offer terms (APR band, term length) for consumers who have completed a soft-pull pre-qualification.

sections.label: Training Data Summary
sections.excerpt: Vendor packet incomplete — Training Data Summary not yet received. Requested 2026-04-30; follow-up sent.

sections.label: Known Limitations
sections.excerpt: Limited training data on borrowers with no prior credit history; conservative default recommendations applied.

sections.label: Trade-Secret Withholding Notice
sections.excerpt: Algorithmic decision boundary parameters withheld; deployer notified per §1702(1)(d).

sections.label: Intended Use Statement
sections.excerpt: Skills-Match ranks candidates against a job requisition. Output is a relative rank within the applicant pool; deployers are responsible for the threshold above which recruiters review candidates manually.

sections.label: Training Data Summary
sections.excerpt: Trained on 18M resume/req pairs from 312 deployer customers across professional services, financial services, healthcare. NYC LL144 bias audit conducted Q1 2026 by independent auditor (BABL AI); audit summary attached.

sections.label: Trade-Secret Withholding Notice
sections.excerpt: Skill-graph weighting and synonym ontology withheld; deployer notified per §1702(1)(d).

pending_diff.summary: Workday has shipped Skills-Match v2.2 — changes to the tenure-pattern signal. New §1702 packet + updated independent bias audit attached.

sections.label: Training Data Summary
sections.excerpt: Vendor packet incomplete — IL BIPA-specific consent flow documentation not yet received. Requested 2026-05-01; follow-up sent 2026-05-10.

sections.label: Intended Use Statement
sections.excerpt: Captive auto-loan underwriting scorer is intended for use in evaluating applications for credit to purchase or lease Pinetop vehicles. Output is a probability-of-default score in [0,1] combined with a model-recommended decision band; deployers (Pinetop MAC) set the auto-decline threshold and human-review gate.

sections.label: Training Data Summary
sections.excerpt: Trained on 12.4M Pinetop captive-finance applications (2018–2024). v11 introduced — for opt-in connected-vehicle owners — telematics-derived 'behavioral risk' features (harsh-braking rate, late-night driving share, geofence variance) drawn from the consumer's prior Pinetop vehicle.

sections.label: Known Limitations
sections.excerpt: Telematics features available only for prior connected-vehicle owners; backfilled with median for new-to-Pinetop applicants. Performance not validated on subleased or fleet-derived telematics signals.

sections.label: Risk Mitigation Practices
sections.excerpt: Vendor packet incomplete — secondary-use opt-in attestation for telematics features not yet provided. Requested 2026-05-01; follow-up sent 2026-05-15. This is the gap surfaced in the active CPPA inquiry.

sections.label: Trade-Secret Withholding Notice
sections.excerpt: Feature-weight matrix and telematics signal definitions withheld as trade secret per §1702(1); deployer notified per §1702(1)(d). Note: telematics feature presence is itself disclosed; only the weighting is withheld.

pending_diff.summary: Pinetop MAC has published v11.4.1 — telematics 'behavioral risk' features REMOVED pending privacy review. New §1702 packet attached.

sections.label: Intended Use Statement
sections.excerpt: Real-time driver-attention scoring for ADAS safety. Output drives in-cabin alerts and, optionally, aggregated cohort labels written back to the OEM's connected-vehicle platform.

sections.label: Known Limitations
sections.excerpt: Cadence and gaze-vector signals exhibit measurable variance across demographic groups; in-vehicle calibration mitigates but does not eliminate. Performance degraded under prescription glasses with anti-reflective coatings (see Appendix C).
